 {"id":"rich_rust-hsu","title":"Add Unit Tests for Segment Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/segment.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Segment Construction
- [ ] Segment::new(text, style)
- [ ] Segment::styled(text, style)
- [ ] Segment::plain(text)
- [ ] Segment::line()
- [ ] Segment::control(codes)

### Segment Properties
- [ ] cell_length() for ASCII
- [ ] cell_length() for wide characters
- [ ] cell_length() for control (should be 0)
- [ ] is_control()
- [ ] is_empty()

### split_at_cell()
- [ ] Split ASCII at position
- [ ] Split with wide character before split
- [ ] Split with wide character at split point
- [ ] Split control segment (no-op)

### Segment Operations
- [ ] apply_style() with pre and post styles
- [ ] split_lines() single line
- [ ] split_lines() multiple newlines
- [ ] split_lines() with control segments
- [ ] simplify() merges same-style
- [ ] simplify() preserves different styles
- [ ] divide() at single cut
- [ ] divide() at multiple cuts
- [ ] adjust_line_length() padding
- [ ] adjust_line_length() truncation

### Vertical Alignment
- [ ] align_top()
- [ ] align_bottom()
- [ ] align_middle()
- [ ] Content taller than height

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/segment.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for segment.rs. All operations verified.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All segment tests must log:
1. Input segment construction
2. Cell length calculations
3. Split/divide operations with positions
4. Resulting segments

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_segment_split() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let seg = Segment::new("hello world", Some(Style::new().bold()));
    debug!(text = %seg.text, cell_len = seg.cell_length(), "Original segment");
    
    let (left, right) = seg.split_at_cell(5);
    debug!(
        left_text = %left.text, left_len = left.cell_length(),
        right_text = %right.text, right_len = right.cell_length(),
        "Split result"
    );
    
    assert_eq!(left.text, "hello");
    info!("Segment split PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:46.465004269-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:00.985252935-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-hsu","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.730096285-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-jeo","title":"Validate Alignment against RICH_SPEC Section 11","description":"## Objective
Verify the Alignment implementation in src/renderables/align.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 11 exactly.

## Background
Alignment provides horizontal and vertical positioning of content within a given space. Used by Table cells, Panel content, and standalone alignment.

## Validation Checklist

### 11.1 AlignMethod Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Left, Center, Right
- [ ] Verify Default maps to Left

### 11.2 VerticalAlignMethod Enum
- [ ] Verify variants: Top, Middle, Bottom
- [ ] Verify Default maps to Top

### 11.3 Align Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: renderable, align, vertical, width, height, pad
- [ ] Verify Align::new(renderable) constructor
- [ ] Verify builder methods

### 11.4 align_text Function
- [ ] Verify align_text(text, width, align) function
- [ ] Verify Left: no padding
- [ ] Verify Right: left padding
- [ ] Verify Center: balanced padding (left has floor, right has ceil)

### 11.5 Vertical Alignment
- [ ] Verify Top: content at top, padding at bottom
- [ ] Verify Bottom: padding at top, content at bottom
- [ ] Verify Middle: balanced padding

### 11.6 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test content wider than width (no padding)
- [ ] Test content taller than height (no padding)
- [ ] Test odd-width centering

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/align.rs (primary)
- src/segment.rs (align_top/middle/bottom)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 11","acceptance_criteria":"All alignment methods work correctly including edge cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:40.251671454-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:40.251671454-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-jeo","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.393871298-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-k5a","title":"Validate Padding Component","description":"Validate Padding component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Padding adds whitespace around renderable content on all four sides.

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Padding struct - wraps any renderable
2. PaddingDimensions - (top, right, bottom, left) tuple

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] PaddingDimensions from single value (all sides equal)
- [ ] PaddingDimensions from (vertical, horizontal) tuple
- [ ] PaddingDimensions from (top, right, bottom, left) tuple
- [ ] Top padding adds blank lines before content
- [ ] Bottom padding adds blank lines after content
- [ ] Left padding prepends spaces to each line
- [ ] Right padding appends spaces to each line (if expand=true)
- [ ] Padding respects available width constraints
- [ ] Nested padding accumulates correctly
- [ ] Zero padding edge case (no-op)
- [ ] Asymmetric padding (e.g., 0,2,0,2 for horizontal only)

KEY BEHAVIORS:
- Left/right padding affects cell width calculations
- Top/bottom padding affects line count
- Padding content is unstyled (plain spaces)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/padding.rs
- Python rich/padding.py for reference","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:34.926373557-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:34.926373557-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-kko","title":"Validate Style System against RICH_SPEC Section 2","description":"## Objective
Verify the Style implementation in src/style.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 2 exactly.

## Background
The Style system is fundamental to rich_rust - it encapsulates visual attributes (colors, bold, italic, etc.) that are applied to text. The RICH_SPEC defines 13 attributes using bitflags and specific combine/override semantics.

## Validation Checklist

### 2.1 Attributes Bitflags
- [ ] Verify all 13 attributes defined: BOLD, DIM, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, BLINK, BLINK2, REVERSE, CONCEAL, STRIKE, UNDERLINE2, FRAME, ENCIRCLE, OVERLINE
- [ ] Verify bit positions match spec (BOLD=1, DIM=2, ITALIC=4, etc.)
- [ ] Verify set() and unset() methods work correctly
- [ ] Verify is_set() method returns correct boolean

### 2.2 Style Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: color (Option<Color>), bgcolor (Option<Color>), attributes (Attributes), link (Option<String>), meta (Option<HashMap>)
- [ ] Verify Style::null() creates empty style (all None, empty attributes)
- [ ] Verify Style::new() creates null style
- [ ] Verify builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), strike(), dim(), reverse(), blink(), conceal(), overline()
- [ ] Verify color() and on() methods for foreground/background colors
- [ ] Verify link() method for hyperlinks (OSC 8 support)

### 2.3 Style Combination
- [ ] Verify combine(&other) merges styles with other taking precedence
- [ ] Test: null.combine(bold) = bold
- [ ] Test: bold.combine(italic) = bold+italic
- [ ] Test: red.combine(blue) = blue (color override)
- [ ] Verify attribute union: self.attributes | other.attributes

### 2.4 Style Parsing
- [ ] Verify Style::parse() handles named colors
- [ ] Verify attribute keywords: bold, dim, italic, underline, blink, reverse, conceal, strike, overline
- [ ] Verify 'on' keyword for background
- [ ] Verify 'not' prefix for unsetting
- [ ] Verify 'link=URL' syntax for hyperlinks

### 2.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] Verify render_to_ansi() produces correct SGR sequences
- [ ] Verify color system downgrade: TrueColor->EightBit->Standard
- [ ] Verify reset code (0) when style is null

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified and documented. Any discrepancies logged as separate bug/task beads.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.607570332-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.607570332-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-kko","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.695741246-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-koa","title":"Validate Syntax Highlighting Module (feature-gated)","description":"Validate the Syntax module (requires "syntax" feature flag) against Python Rich Syntax behavior.

FEATURE FLAG: syntax (requires syntect crate)

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Syntax struct construction and configuration
2. Theme support and customization
3. Line number rendering
4. Language detection and explicit selection
5. Code block borders and padding

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Syntax::new() accepts source code and language
- [ ] Theme selection (default themes work)
- [ ] Line numbers option (show_line_numbers)
- [ ] Line range selection (start_line, end_line)
- [ ] Word wrap behavior within code blocks
- [ ] Background color styling
- [ ] Indent guide characters (if supported)
- [ ] Tab expansion to spaces
- [ ] SyntaxError type for failures

RENDERING TESTS:
- [ ] Renders to styled Segments correctly
- [ ] Colors match syntect theme
- [ ] Line numbers aligned properly
- [ ] Handles unknown languages gracefully

EDGE CASES:
- [ ] Empty source code
- [ ] Very long lines
- [ ] Unknown language fallback
- [ ] Source with no syntax highlighting (plain text)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/syntax.rs
- Python rich/syntax.py for reference","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:06.20618533-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:06.20618533-05:00","labels":["feature-gated","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-ms5","title":"Verify Table Width Collapse Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify collapse_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table content exceeds available width, collapse_widths() must reduce column widths proportionally while respecting minimum widths. This is a complex algorithm with many edge cases.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
collapse_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = current_total - available_width
2. Distribute reduction proportionally to (current - minimum)
3. Never reduce below minimum width
4. Handle columns that hit minimum (redistribute to others)

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [40, 30, 30] = 100
       minimums [10, 10, 10]
       available width = 70
       excess = 30

Step 1: Calculate reducible space per column
  col0: 40-10 = 30 reducible
  col1: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  col2: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  total reducible = 70

Step 2: Distribute reduction proportionally
  col0 reduces by: 30 * (30/70) = ~12.9
  col1 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6
  col2 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6

Result: [27, 21, 22] (with rounding)
```

### Test Cases
1. All columns have same reducible space
2. One column at minimum (can't reduce further)
3. All columns at minimum
4. Single column table
5. Very narrow width (forces all to minimum)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (collapse_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"collapse_widths matches spec. All test cases pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:58.81508283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:58.81508283-05:00","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.781926826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.046952534-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
